evals.prompt: Initialize a new repository with the standard AI agent workflow structure. Need .agents/skills, plans/, agents-docs/, and proper symlinks for Claude, Gemini, and Qwen.
evals.expected_output: Created complete directory structure (.agents/skills, plans, agents-docs, scripts, src/ subdirectories), setup-skills.sh script for creating symlinks, quality_gate.sh for pre-commit validation, and working symlinks in .claude/skills, .gemini/skills, .qwen/skills.
assertions: setup-skills.sh script is created and executable

evals.prompt: Set up pre-commit hooks and quality gates for this repo. Should validate skills, run typecheck, and lint before commits.
evals.expected_output: Created pre-commit-hook.sh with global hooks check, quality_gate.sh running typecheck/lint/validate-skills, validate-skills.sh checking SKILL.md frontmatter. All scripts are executable and quality gate fails fast on first error.
assertions: pre-commit-hook.sh is created in scripts/ and is executable
quality_gate.sh runs typecheck, lint, and validate-skills
All shell scripts include set -euo pipefail
Quality gate uses fail-fast behavior (exits on first error)

evals.prompt: Bootstrap a Vite + TypeScript project for a GitHub Gist CRUD app. Include proper tsconfig, vite.config, and project structure with src/ subdirectories.
evals.expected_output: Configured vite.config.ts with TypeScript support, tsconfig.json with strict mode, src/ directory with subdirectories (components, lib, styles, tokens), index.html entry point, and package.json with Vite and TypeScript dependencies.
assertions: vite.config.ts is created with TypeScript plugin
tsconfig.json has strict mode enabled (strict: true, noImplicitAny: true)
src/ has subdirectories: components, lib, styles, tokens
index.html is the Vite entry point
package.json includes vite, typescript, and @types packages

evals.prompt: Create the AGENTS.md file for this project following the standard template. It should include mission, stack, skills, code style rules, and reference docs.
evals.expected_output: A comprehensive AGENTS.md at repo root with frontmatter, mission statement, stack table, skills discovery rules, code style and naming rules, setup/dev/test/build commands, and reference docs section. Follows agents.md specification format.
assertions: Contains mission statement describing the app purpose
Stack table lists technologies with rationale
Includes code style rules (naming, file limits, conventions)
Contains reference docs section with official documentation links

evals.prompt: Set up the scripts directory with all utility scripts needed for the project: setup-skills.sh, quality_gate.sh, and validate-skills.sh. Make them all shellcheck compliant.
evals.expected_output: Scripts directory with setup-skills.sh (creates agent symlinks), quality_gate.sh (runs typecheck, lint, tests), validate-skills.sh (checks all SKILL.md files for valid frontmatter). All scripts start with #!/usr/bin/env bash and set -euo pipefail, pass shellcheck.

evals.prompt: Initialize the plans/ directory with a project charter and initial v1 scope document. The charter should define the project vision, v1 vs v2 boundaries, and success metrics.
evals.expected_output: plans/ directory with 000-project-charter.md containing project vision, success metrics, and v1/v2 scope boundaries. A v1 scope plan file detailing what features are in scope for v1 (PAT auth, IndexedDB, gist CRUD, PWA, Capacitor) and what is deferred to v2 (OAuth, backend, real-time).
assertions: Charter defines v1 vs v2 scope boundaries
A v1 scope plan details in-scope features

evals.prompt: Set up a .gitignore appropriate for a Vite + TypeScript + Capacitor + Node.js project. Include all generated files, build artifacts, and IDE files.
evals.expected_output: A .gitignore covering node_modules/, dist/, .vite/, *.tsbuildinfo, .DS_Store, IDE files (.vscode/, .idea/), Capacitor native platforms (android/), environment files (.env), and OS-generated files.

evals.prompt: Add a README.md that documents the project for both humans and AI agents. Should include project description, tech stack, setup instructions, how AI agents should use AGENTS.md, and links to official docs.
evals.expected_output: README.md with project description, badge section, tech stack summary, quick start commands (ppnpm install, pnpm run dev), link to AGENTS.md for AI agents, and links to Vite, GitHub API, Capacitor, and Playwright documentation.
assertions: README.md includes project description and tech stack
Quick start commands are documented (install, dev, build)
AGENTS.md is linked for AI agent workflow
Links to official docs for Vite, GitHub API, Capacitor
README mentions PWA and offline-first capabilities
